```{r}
  fit_summary_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(nested, "fit_summary"))
  cv_preds_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(nested, "cv_preds"))

  fit_comparison_all <- fit_summary_all %>%
    dplyr::select(
      experiment, formula_spec,
      n_fits_total, n_fit_failed, n_predict_failed,
      n_nonposdef_hessian_fits, n_nonzero_convergence_fits, n_warning_fits,
      prop_fit_failed, prop_predict_failed,
      prop_nonposdef_hessian, prop_nonzero_convergence, prop_warning,
      rmse_overall, mae_overall
    ) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = formula_spec,
      values_from = -experiment,
      names_sep = "__"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      diff_prop_fit_failed_with_minus_without =
        prop_fit_failed__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction -
        prop_fit_failed__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction,

      diff_prop_predict_failed_with_minus_without =
        prop_predict_failed__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction -
        prop_predict_failed__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction,

      diff_prop_nonposdef_hessian_with_minus_without =
        prop_nonposdef_hessian__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction -
        prop_nonposdef_hessian__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction,

      diff_prop_nonzero_convergence_with_minus_without =
        prop_nonzero_convergence__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction -
        prop_nonzero_convergence__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction,

      diff_prop_warning_with_minus_without =
        prop_warning__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction -
        prop_warning__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction,

      delta_rmse_overall_with_minus_without =
        rmse_overall__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction -
        rmse_overall__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction,

      delta_mae_overall_with_minus_without =
        mae_overall__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction -
        mae_overall__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction
    )

  aoi_mean_comparison_all <- aoi_prediction_all %>%
    dplyr::group_by(experiment, aggr_level, AOI, formula_spec) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      n_topics = dplyr::n(),
      mean_rmse = safe_mean(rmse),
      median_rmse = median(rmse, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_mae = safe_mean(mae),
      median_mae = median(mae, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = formula_spec,
      values_from = c(n_topics, mean_rmse, median_rmse, mean_mae, median_mae),
      names_sep = "__"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      comparable =
        !is.na(mean_rmse__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction) &
        !is.na(mean_rmse__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction),

      winner_mean_rmse = dplyr::case_when(
        !comparable ~ NA_character_,
        mean_rmse__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction < mean_rmse__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction ~ "with_AOI_nLetters_interaction",
        mean_rmse__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction > mean_rmse__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction ~ "no_AOI_nLetters_interaction",
        TRUE ~ "equal"
      ),

      winner_mean_mae = dplyr::case_when(
        !comparable ~ NA_character_,
        mean_mae__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction < mean_mae__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction ~ "with_AOI_nLetters_interaction",
        mean_mae__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction > mean_mae__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction ~ "no_AOI_nLetters_interaction",
        TRUE ~ "equal"
      ),

      delta_mean_rmse_with_minus_without =
        mean_rmse__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction - mean_rmse__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction,

      delta_mean_mae_with_minus_without =
        mean_mae__with_AOI_nLetters_interaction - mean_mae__no_AOI_nLetters_interaction
    )

  aoi_winner_counts_all <- aoi_mean_comparison_all %>%
    dplyr::select(experiment, winner_mean_rmse, winner_mean_mae) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c(winner_mean_rmse, winner_mean_mae),
      names_to = "metric",
      values_to = "winner"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(winner)) %>%
    dplyr::count(experiment, metric, winner, name = "n_aoi")

  list(
    nested = nested,
    cv_preds_all = cv_preds_all,
    fit_level_all = fit_level_all,
    aoi_prediction_all = aoi_prediction_all,
    aoi_comparison_all = aoi_comparison_all,
    fit_summary_all = fit_summary_all,
    fit_comparison_all = fit_comparison_all,
    aoi_mean_comparison_all = aoi_mean_comparison_all,
    aoi_winner_counts_all = aoi_winner_counts_all
  )
}

# =========================================================
# AUSFÜHREN
# =========================================================

res_gamma_aoi_length_interaction_aggregated <- run_topic_loo_compare_all_aggregated(
  dfs = dfs,
  response = "FRT",
  experiments_with_speaker = experiments_with_speaker,
  pred_re_form = NULL
)

# =========================================================
# OUTPUTS
# =========================================================

# AOI x Topic direkter Vergleich im aggregated Modell
#round_df_4(res_gamma_aoi_length_interaction_aggregated$aoi_comparison_all)

# AOI x Topic RMSE / MAE je Spezifikation
#round_df_4(res_gamma_aoi_length_interaction_aggregated$aoi_prediction_all)

# Fitprobleme und Gesamtgüte
#round_df_4(res_gamma_aoi_length_interaction_aggregated$fit_summary_all)

# Direkter Vergleich mit vs ohne AOI:nLetters
#round_df_4(res_gamma_aoi_length_interaction_aggregated$fit_comparison_all)

# AOI-weise Mittelwerte über Topics
round_df_4(res_gamma_aoi_length_interaction_aggregated$aoi_mean_comparison_all)

# Winner counts
round_df_4(res_gamma_aoi_length_interaction_aggregated$aoi_winner_counts_all)

# Detail pro Fit
#round_df_4(res_gamma_aoi_length_interaction_aggregated$fit_level_all)
```
Methodisch wurde geprüft, wie gut die Modelle auf neue Topics innerhalb eines bereits definierten AOI-Clusters generalisieren. Dafür wurde jeweils ein Topic vollständig aus dem Training entfernt, das Modell auf den verbleibenden Topics des Clusters geschätzt und anschließend für das ausgelassene Topic vorhergesagt. Die Vorhersage wurde dann AOI-spezifisch ausgewertet. Der zentrale Vergleich war also nicht, wie gut ein Modell die Trainingsdaten beschreibt, sondern ob ein zusätzlicher AOI-spezifischer Längeneffekt die Vorhersage für unbeobachtete Items innerhalb derselben Clusterstruktur verbessert.

Die Vorhersagemuster sprechen insgesamt gegen einen allgemeinen Nutzen dieses zusätzlichen AOI-spezifischen Längeneffekts. In den meisten AOIs verschlechtert er die Generalisierung auf neue Topics oder bringt höchstens minimale Änderungen. Nur in einzelnen AOIs gibt es lokale Verbesserungen, die aber kein konsistentes Muster über Experimente und Cluster hinweg ergeben. Das deutet darauf hin, dass der zusätzliche Freiheitsgrad meist eher cluster- oder item-spezifische Besonderheiten aufgreift, statt eine robuste, auf neue Topics übertragbare Struktur zu lernen. Entsprechend reicht für die Hauptanalyse ein einfacherer globaler Längeneffekt aus; AOI-spezifische Längeneffekte wären eher etwas für gezielte Nachanalysen einzelner auffälliger Cluster.


Wir überprüfen die Anwendung von Splines zuerst mit penalized Splines
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(mgcv)

# =========================================================
# INPUT
# Erwartet vorhandene Datensätze:
# c_bed, c_ein, n_bed, n_ein, CS
# =========================================================

dfs <- list(
  c_bed = c_bed,
  c_ein = c_ein,
  n_bed = n_bed,
  n_ein = n_ein,
  CS    = CS
)

experiments_with_speaker <- c("n_bed", "n_ein", "CS")

# =========================================================
# HELFER
# =========================================================

round_df_4 <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))
}

safe_plain_df <- function(df) {
  out <- as.data.frame(df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  rownames(out) <- NULL
  out
}

collapse_unique <- function(x) {
  x <- unique(stats::na.omit(x))
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_character_)
  paste(x, collapse = " | ")
}

# =========================================================
# DATEN VORBEREITEN
# =========================================================

prep_cluster_data_pspline <- function(df,
                                      aggr_value,
                                      response = "FRT",
                                      use_speaker = FALSE) {
  needed <- c(response, "nLetters", "Participant", "Topic", "condition", "AOI", "aggr_level")
  if (use_speaker) needed <- c(needed, "speaker")

  missing_cols <- setdiff(needed, names(df))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop(
      "Fehlende Variablen: ",
      paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }

  out <- df %>%
    filter(as.character(aggr_level) == as.character(aggr_value)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[response]])) %>%
    filter(.data[[response]] > 0) %>%
    filter(!is.na(nLetters)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Participant), !is.na(Topic), !is.na(condition), !is.na(AOI), !is.na(aggr_level)) %>%
    mutate(
      Participant = factor(Participant),
      Topic = factor(Topic),
      condition = factor(condition),
      AOI = factor(AOI),
      aggr_level = factor(aggr_level)
    ) %>%
    droplevels()

  if (use_speaker) {
    out <- out %>%
      filter(!is.na(speaker)) %>%
      mutate(speaker = factor(speaker))
  }

  safe_plain_df(out)
}

# =========================================================
# FORMELN
# Kein AOI-spezifischer Spline, nur gemeinsamer s(nLetters)
# =========================================================

make_pspline_formula <- function(data,
                                 response = "FRT",
                                 use_speaker = FALSE,
                                 k_spline = 5) {
  n_aoi <- nlevels(droplevels(data$AOI))
  n_unique_len <- dplyr::n_distinct(data$nLetters)

  if (n_unique_len < 4) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  k_eff <- min(k_spline, n_unique_len - 1)
  k_eff <- max(k_eff, 4)

  param_part <- if (n_aoi > 1) {
    if (use_speaker) {
      "AOI * speaker * condition"
    } else {
      "AOI * condition"
    }
  } else {
    if (use_speaker) {
      "speaker * condition"
    } else {
      "condition"
    }
  }

  smooth_part <- paste0("s(nLetters, bs='ps', k=", k_eff, ")")
  re_part <- "s(Participant, bs='re') + s(Topic, bs='re')"

  as.formula(
    paste(response, "~", param_part, "+", smooth_part, "+", re_part)
  )
}

# =========================================================
# FIT
# =========================================================

fit_pspline_gamma_capture <- function(data,
                                      formula,
                                      re_edf_tol = 1e-4) {
  warns <- character(0)

  fit <- withCallingHandlers(
    tryCatch(
      mgcv::gam(
        formula = formula,
        data = data,
        family = Gamma(link = "log"),
        method = "REML",
        select = TRUE
      ),
      error = function(e) e
    ),
    warning = function(w) {
      warns <<- c(warns, conditionMessage(w))
      invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
    }
  )

  if (inherits(fit, "error")) {
    return(list(
      fit_ok = FALSE,
      fit = NULL,
      fit_error = conditionMessage(fit),
      fit_warning = collapse_unique(warns),
      rank_deficient = NA,
      re_participant_zero = NA,
      re_topic_zero = NA,
      re_zero_any = NA,
      spline_edf = NA_real_,
      aic = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  s_tab <- tryCatch(
    as.data.frame(summary(fit)$s.table),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(s_tab) || nrow(s_tab) == 0) {
    s_tab <- data.frame(edf = numeric(0))
    rownames(s_tab) <- character(0)
  }

  s_tab$smooth_label <- rownames(s_tab)

  part_edf <- s_tab %>%
    filter(grepl("^s\\(Participant\\)", smooth_label)) %>%
    summarise(edf = sum(edf, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    pull(edf)

  topic_edf <- s_tab %>%
    filter(grepl("^s\\(Topic\\)", smooth_label)) %>%
    summarise(edf = sum(edf, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    pull(edf)

  spline_edf <- s_tab %>%
    filter(grepl("^s\\(nLetters\\)", smooth_label)) %>%
    summarise(edf = sum(edf, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    pull(edf)

  part_edf <- if (length(part_edf) == 0) NA_real_ else part_edf
  topic_edf <- if (length(topic_edf) == 0) NA_real_ else topic_edf
  spline_edf <- if (length(spline_edf) == 0) NA_real_ else spline_edf

  re_participant_zero <- !is.na(part_edf) && part_edf < re_edf_tol
  re_topic_zero <- !is.na(topic_edf) && topic_edf < re_edf_tol

  rank_deficient <- FALSE
  if (!is.null(fit$rank) && !is.null(coef(fit))) {
    rank_deficient <- fit$rank < length(coef(fit))
  }

  list(
    fit_ok = TRUE,
    fit = fit,
    fit_error = NA_character_,
    fit_warning = collapse_unique(warns),
    rank_deficient = rank_deficient,
    re_participant_zero = re_participant_zero,
    re_topic_zero = re_topic_zero,
    re_zero_any = re_participant_zero | re_topic_zero,
    spline_edf = spline_edf,
    aic = AIC(fit)
  )
}

# =========================================================
# EIN CLUSTER
# =========================================================

run_one_cluster_pspline <- function(df,
                                    exp_name,
                                    aggr_value,
                                    response = "FRT",
                                    use_speaker = FALSE,
                                    k_spline = 5,
                                    re_edf_tol = 1e-4) {
  dat <- prep_cluster_data_pspline(
    df = df,
    aggr_value = aggr_value,
    response = response,
    use_speaker = use_speaker
  )

  n_aoi <- nlevels(droplevels(dat$AOI))
  n_uniqueLetters <- dplyr::n_distinct(dat$nLetters)

  base_info <- tibble(
    exp = exp_name,
    aggr_level = as.character(aggr_value),
    n_obs = nrow(dat),
    n_aoi_in_cluster = n_aoi,
    n_uniqueLetters = n_uniqueLetters,
    use_speaker = use_speaker
  )

  if (nrow(dat) == 0) {
    return(
      base_info %>%
        mutate(
          fit_ok = FALSE,
          fit_failed = TRUE,
          rank_deficient = NA,
          re_participant_zero = NA,
          re_topic_zero = NA,
          re_zero_any = NA,
          has_warning = TRUE,
          fail_reason = "empty_cluster_after_filtering",
          warning_msg = "empty_cluster_after_filtering",
          spline_edf = NA_real_,
          aic = NA_real_
        ) %>%
        round_df_4()
    )
  }

  fml <- make_pspline_formula(
    data = dat,
    response = response,
    use_speaker = use_speaker,
    k_spline = k_spline
  )

  if (is.null(fml)) {
    return(
      base_info %>%
        mutate(
          fit_ok = FALSE,
          fit_failed = TRUE,
          rank_deficient = NA,
          re_participant_zero = NA,
          re_topic_zero = NA,
          re_zero_any = NA,
          has_warning = TRUE,
          fail_reason = "too_few_unique_nLetters_values",
          warning_msg = "too_few_unique_nLetters_values",
          spline_edf = NA_real_,
          aic = NA_real_
        ) %>%
        round_df_4()
    )
  }

  fit_out <- fit_pspline_gamma_capture(
    data = dat,
    formula = fml,
    re_edf_tol = re_edf_tol
  )

  base_info %>%
    mutate(
      fit_ok = fit_out$fit_ok,
      fit_failed = !fit_out$fit_ok,
      rank_deficient = fit_out$rank_deficient,
      re_participant_zero = fit_out$re_participant_zero,
      re_topic_zero = fit_out$re_topic_zero,
      re_zero_any = fit_out$re_zero_any,
      has_warning = !is.na(fit_out$fit_warning),
      fail_reason = fit_out$fit_error,
      warning_msg = fit_out$fit_warning,
      spline_edf = fit_out$spline_edf,
      aic = fit_out$aic
    ) %>%
    round_df_4()
}

# =========================================================
# ALLE CLUSTER EINES EXPERIMENTS
# =========================================================

run_one_experiment_pspline <- function(df,
                                       exp_name,
                                       response = "FRT",
                                       use_speaker = FALSE,
                                       k_spline = 5,
                                       re_edf_tol = 1e-4) {
  aggr_vals <- unique(as.character(df$aggr_level))

  cluster_results <- purrr::map_dfr(aggr_vals, function(ag) {
    run_one_cluster_pspline(
      df = df,
      exp_name = exp_name,
      aggr_value = ag,
      response = response,
      use_speaker = use_speaker,
      k_spline = k_spline,
      re_edf_tol = re_edf_tol
    )
  }) %>%
    arrange(aggr_level) %>%
    select(
      exp,
      aggr_level,
      n_uniqueLetters,
      spline_edf
    ) %>%
    round_df_4()

  summary_results <- cluster_results %>%
    summarise(
      exp = exp_name,
      n_clusters = dplyr::n(),
      mean_spline_edf = mean(spline_edf, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_spline_edf = median(spline_edf, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    round_df_4()

  list(
    cluster_results = cluster_results,
    summary_results = summary_results
  )
}

# =========================================================
# ÜBER ALLE EXPERIMENTE
# =========================================================

run_cluster_psplines_all <- function(dfs,
                                     response = "FRT",
                                     experiments_with_speaker = c("n_bed", "n_ein", "CS"),
                                     k_spline = 5,
                                     re_edf_tol = 1e-4) {
  nested <- purrr::imap(dfs, function(df, exp_name) {
    run_one_experiment_pspline(
      df = df,
      exp_name = exp_name,
      response = response,
      use_speaker = exp_name %in% experiments_with_speaker,
      k_spline = k_spline,
      re_edf_tol = re_edf_tol
    )
  })

  cluster_results_all <- bind_rows(purrr::map(nested, "cluster_results")) %>%
    arrange(exp, aggr_level) %>%
    select(
      exp,
      aggr_level,
      n_uniqueLetters,
      spline_edf
    ) %>%
    round_df_4()

  summary_results_all <- bind_rows(purrr::map(nested, "summary_results")) %>%
    select(
      exp,
      n_clusters,
      mean_spline_edf,
      median_spline_edf
    ) %>%
    round_df_4()

  overall <- cluster_results_all %>%
    summarise(
      n_clusters = dplyr::n(),
      mean_spline_edf = mean(spline_edf, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_spline_edf = median(spline_edf, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    round_df_4()

  list(
    nested = nested,
    cluster_results_all = cluster_results_all,
    summary_results_all = summary_results_all,
    overall = overall
  )
}

# =========================================================
# RECHNEN
# =========================================================

res_cluster_pspline_gamma <- run_cluster_psplines_all(
  dfs = dfs,
  response = "FRT",
  experiments_with_speaker = experiments_with_speaker,
  k_spline = 5,
  re_edf_tol = 1e-4
)

# =========================================================
# OUTPUTS
# =========================================================

# Alle Cluster
round_df_4(res_cluster_pspline_gamma$cluster_results_all)

# Zusammenfassung pro Experiment
round_df_4(res_cluster_pspline_gamma$summary_results_all)

# Gesamtübersicht
round_df_4(res_cluster_pspline_gamma$overall)
```

Die clustergetrennten penalized-spline-Modelle mit gemeinsamem Spline für nLetters waren in allen Datensätzen technisch stabil fitbar. Inhaltlich zeigte sich jedoch kein einheitliches Muster. Während in c_ein kaum Evidenz für einen nichtlinearen Längeneffekt vorlag und n_bed weitgehend mit einem annähernd linearen Verlauf vereinbar war, zeigten n_ein, CS und Teile von c_bed EDF-Werte klar über 1 und damit Hinweise auf Nichtlinearität. Ein gemeinsamer penalized spline ist damit als flexible Sensitivitätsanalyse gut vertretbar, rechtfertigt aber auf Basis dieser Ergebnisse noch keine pauschale Ablösung des linearen nLetters-Terms in allen Clustern.

Wegen der starken Designgebundenheit von nLetters an AOI x condition x Topic und der fehlenden konsistenten Evidenz für zusätzliche Flexibilität sollte der Längeneffekt parsimonisch modelliert werden. Ein gemeinsamer linearer nLetters-Term nutzt den Aggregationsvorteil, bleibt über alle Experimente hinweg anwendbar und vermeidet die zusätzliche Komplexität flexibler oder AOI-spezifischer Längenmodellierung, die in den vorliegenden Daten keinen durchgängig überzeugenden Mehrwert zeigt.

Da bereits die alleinstehenden splines ohne AOI interaktion fragwürdig sind, versuchen wir gar nicht die Splines by AOI zu rechtfertigen.

Wir testen noch performance in einem Topic - LOO von penalized splines vs nLetters.
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(glmmTMB)
library(mgcv)

# =========================================================
# INPUT
# Erwartet vorhandene Datensätze:
# c_bed, c_ein, n_bed, n_ein, CS
# =========================================================

dfs <- list(
  c_bed = c_bed,
  c_ein = c_ein,
  n_bed = n_bed,
  n_ein = n_ein,
  CS    = CS
)

experiments_with_speaker <- c("n_bed", "n_ein", "CS")

# =========================================================
# HELFER
# =========================================================

round_df_4 <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))
}

safe_plain_df <- function(df) {
  out <- as.data.frame(df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  rownames(out) <- NULL
  out
}

safe_mean <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  mean(x)
}

safe_rmse <- function(sq_error) {
  sq_error <- sq_error[is.finite(sq_error)]
  if (length(sq_error) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  sqrt(mean(sq_error))
}

# =========================================================
# DATEN VORBEREITEN
# =========================================================

prep_topicloo_data <- function(df,
                               response = "FRT",
                               use_speaker = FALSE) {
  needed <- c(response, "nLetters", "Participant", "Topic", "condition", "AOI", "aggr_level")
  if (use_speaker) needed <- c(needed, "speaker")

  missing_cols <- setdiff(needed, names(df))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Fehlende Variablen: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }

  out <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[response]])) %>%
    filter(.data[[response]] > 0) %>%
    filter(!is.na(nLetters)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Participant), !is.na(Topic), !is.na(condition), !is.na(AOI), !is.na(aggr_level))

  if (use_speaker) {
    out <- out %>% filter(!is.na(speaker))
  }

  out <- out %>%
    mutate(
      Participant = factor(Participant),
      Topic = factor(Topic),
      condition = factor(condition),
      AOI = factor(AOI),
      aggr_level = factor(aggr_level)
    )

  if (use_speaker) {
    out <- out %>% mutate(speaker = factor(speaker))
  }

  safe_plain_df(out)
}

# =========================================================
# FORMELN
# =========================================================

make_linear_formula <- function(data,
                                response = "FRT",
                                use_speaker = FALSE) {
  n_aoi <- nlevels(droplevels(data$AOI))

  rhs <- if (n_aoi > 1) {
    if (use_speaker) {
      "AOI * speaker * condition + nLetters + (1 | Topic) + (1 | Participant)"
    } else {
      "AOI * condition + nLetters + (1 | Topic) + (1 | Participant)"
    }
  } else {
    if (use_speaker) {
      "condition * speaker + nLetters + (1 | Topic) + (1 | Participant)"
    } else {
      "condition + nLetters + (1 | Topic) + (1 | Participant)"
    }
  }

  as.formula(paste(response, "~", rhs))
}

make_pspline_formula <- function(data,
                                 response = "FRT",
                                 use_speaker = FALSE,
                                 k_spline = 5) {
  n_aoi <- nlevels(droplevels(data$AOI))
  n_unique_len <- dplyr::n_distinct(data$nLetters)

  if (n_unique_len < 4) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  k_eff <- min(k_spline, n_unique_len - 1)
  k_eff <- max(k_eff, 4)

  param_part <- if (n_aoi > 1) {
    if (use_speaker) {
      "AOI * speaker * condition"
    } else {
      "AOI * condition"
    }
  } else {
    if (use_speaker) {
      "condition * speaker"
    } else {
      "condition"
    }
  }

  smooth_part <- paste0("s(nLetters, bs='ps', k=", k_eff, ")")
  re_part <- "s(Participant, bs='re') + s(Topic, bs='re')"

  as.formula(
    paste(response, "~", param_part, "+", smooth_part, "+", re_part)
  )
}

# =========================================================
# FITS
# =========================================================

fit_linear_gamma <- function(data, formula) {
  tryCatch(
    glmmTMB::glmmTMB(
      formula = formula,
      data = data,
      family = Gamma(link = "log")
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
}

fit_pspline_gamma <- function(data, formula) {
  tryCatch(
    mgcv::gam(
      formula = formula,
      data = data,
      family = Gamma(link = "log"),
      method = "REML",
      select = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
}

# =========================================================
# EDF EXTRAKTION
# =========================================================

extract_pspline_edf <- function(fit) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NA_real_)

  s_tab <- tryCatch(
    as.data.frame(summary(fit)$s.table),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(s_tab) || nrow(s_tab) == 0) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  s_tab$smooth_label <- rownames(s_tab)

  spline_edf <- s_tab %>%
    filter(grepl("^s\\(nLetters\\)", smooth_label)) %>%
    summarise(edf = sum(edf, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    pull(edf)

  if (length(spline_edf) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  spline_edf
}

# =========================================================
# PREDICT HELFER
# Population-level prediction in beiden Modellen
# =========================================================

predict_linear_gamma <- function(fit, newdata) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(rep(NA_real_, nrow(newdata)))

  pred <- tryCatch(
    predict(
      fit,
      newdata = newdata,
      type = "response",
      re.form = NULL,
      allow.new.levels = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(pred)) return(rep(NA_real_, nrow(newdata)))
  as.numeric(pred)
}

predict_pspline_gamma <- function(fit, newdata) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(rep(NA_real_, nrow(newdata)))

  nd <- newdata
  fit_data <- model.frame(fit)

  if ("Participant" %in% names(nd) && "Participant" %in% names(fit_data)) {
    train_part_levels <- levels(fit_data$Participant)
    if (length(train_part_levels) > 0) {
      nd$Participant <- factor(train_part_levels[1], levels = train_part_levels)
    }
  }

  if ("Topic" %in% names(nd) && "Topic" %in% names(fit_data)) {
    train_topic_levels <- levels(fit_data$Topic)
    if (length(train_topic_levels) > 0) {
      nd$Topic <- factor(train_topic_levels[1], levels = train_topic_levels)
    }
  }

  pred <- tryCatch(
    predict(
      fit,
      newdata = nd,
      type = "response",
      exclude = c("s(Participant)", "s(Topic)")
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(pred)) return(rep(NA_real_, nrow(newdata)))
  as.numeric(pred)
}

# =========================================================
# EIN CLUSTER
# =========================================================

run_topicloo_one_cluster <- function(dat_cluster,
                                     exp_name,
                                     aggr_value,
                                     response = "FRT",
                                     use_speaker = FALSE,
                                     k_spline = 5) {
  topics <- unique(as.character(dat_cluster$Topic))
  n_aoi <- nlevels(droplevels(dat_cluster$AOI))

  res_rows <- map_dfr(topics, function(topic_i) {
    train_df <- dat_cluster %>%
      filter(as.character(Topic) != topic_i) %>%
      droplevels() %>%
      safe_plain_df()

    test_df <- dat_cluster %>%
      filter(as.character(Topic) == topic_i) %>%
      droplevels() %>%
      safe_plain_df()

    linear_formula <- make_linear_formula(
      data = train_df,
      response = response,
      use_speaker = use_speaker
    )

    pspline_formula <- make_pspline_formula(
      data = train_df,
      response = response,
      use_speaker = use_speaker,
      k_spline = k_spline
    )

    fit_linear <- fit_linear_gamma(train_df, linear_formula)

    fit_pspline <- if (is.null(pspline_formula)) {
      NULL
    } else {
      fit_pspline_gamma(
        data = train_df,
        formula = pspline_formula
      )
    }

    pred_linear_num <- predict_linear_gamma(fit_linear, test_df)
    pred_pspline_num <- predict_pspline_gamma(fit_pspline, test_df)

    tibble(
      experiment = exp_name,
      aggr_level = as.character(aggr_value),
      omitted_topic = as.character(topic_i),
      n_aoi_in_cluster = n_aoi,
      n_train = nrow(train_df),
      n_test = nrow(test_df),

      rmse_linear = safe_rmse((test_df[[response]] - pred_linear_num)^2),
      mae_linear = safe_mean(abs(test_df[[response]] - pred_linear_num)),
      rmse_pspline = safe_rmse((test_df[[response]] - pred_pspline_num)^2),
      mae_pspline = safe_mean(abs(test_df[[response]] - pred_pspline_num)),

      delta_rmse_pspline_minus_linear = rmse_pspline - rmse_linear,
      delta_mae_pspline_minus_linear = mae_pspline - mae_linear,

      pspline_edf = extract_pspline_edf(fit_pspline)
    )
  })

  res_rows
}

# =========================================================
# EIN EXPERIMENT
# =========================================================

run_topicloo_one_experiment <- function(df,
                                        exp_name,
                                        response = "FRT",
                                        use_speaker = FALSE,
                                        k_spline = 5) {
  dat <- prep_topicloo_data(
    df = df,
    response = response,
    use_speaker = use_speaker
  )

  aggr_vals <- unique(as.character(dat$aggr_level))

  split_results <- map_dfr(aggr_vals, function(ag) {
    dat_cluster <- dat %>%
      filter(as.character(aggr_level) == ag) %>%
      droplevels() %>%
      safe_plain_df()

    run_topicloo_one_cluster(
      dat_cluster = dat_cluster,
      exp_name = exp_name,
      aggr_value = ag,
      response = response,
      use_speaker = use_speaker,
      k_spline = k_spline
    )
  })

  cluster_summary <- split_results %>%
    group_by(experiment, aggr_level, n_aoi_in_cluster) %>%
    summarise(
      n_topic_splits = n(),
      mean_rmse_linear = safe_mean(rmse_linear),
      mean_rmse_pspline = safe_mean(rmse_pspline),
      mean_mae_linear = safe_mean(mae_linear),
      mean_mae_pspline = safe_mean(mae_pspline),
      mean_delta_rmse_pspline_minus_linear = safe_mean(delta_rmse_pspline_minus_linear),
      mean_delta_mae_pspline_minus_linear = safe_mean(delta_mae_pspline_minus_linear),
      mean_pspline_edf = safe_mean(pspline_edf),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(aggr_level) %>%
    round_df_4()

  experiment_summary <- cluster_summary %>%
    summarise(
      experiment = exp_name,
      n_clusters = n(),
      mean_delta_rmse_pspline_minus_linear =
        safe_mean(mean_delta_rmse_pspline_minus_linear),
      mean_delta_mae_pspline_minus_linear =
        safe_mean(mean_delta_mae_pspline_minus_linear),
      mean_pspline_edf =
        safe_mean(mean_pspline_edf)
    ) %>%
    round_df_4()

  list(
    split_results = round_df_4(split_results),
    cluster_summary = cluster_summary,
    experiment_summary = experiment_summary
  )
}

# =========================================================
# ÜBER ALLE EXPERIMENTE
# =========================================================

run_topicloo_linear_vs_pspline_all <- function(dfs,
                                               response = "FRT",
                                               experiments_with_speaker = c("n_bed", "n_ein", "CS"),
                                               k_spline = 5) {
  nested <- imap(dfs, function(df, exp_name) {
    run_topicloo_one_experiment(
      df = df,
      exp_name = exp_name,
      response = response,
      use_speaker = exp_name %in% experiments_with_speaker,
      k_spline = k_spline
    )
  })

  split_results_all <- bind_rows(map(nested, "split_results")) %>%
    round_df_4()

  cluster_summary_all <- bind_rows(map(nested, "cluster_summary")) %>%
    round_df_4()

  experiment_summary_all <- bind_rows(map(nested, "experiment_summary")) %>%
    round_df_4()

  overall_summary <- cluster_summary_all %>%
    summarise(
      n_clusters = n(),
      mean_delta_rmse_pspline_minus_linear =
        safe_mean(mean_delta_rmse_pspline_minus_linear),
      mean_delta_mae_pspline_minus_linear =
        safe_mean(mean_delta_mae_pspline_minus_linear),
      mean_pspline_edf =
        safe_mean(mean_pspline_edf)
    ) %>%
    round_df_4()

  list(
    nested = nested,
    split_results_all = split_results_all,
    cluster_summary_all = cluster_summary_all,
    experiment_summary_all = experiment_summary_all,
    overall_summary = overall_summary
  )
}

# =========================================================
# RECHNEN
# =========================================================

res_topicloo_linear_vs_pspline <- run_topicloo_linear_vs_pspline_all(
  dfs = dfs,
  response = "FRT",
  experiments_with_speaker = experiments_with_speaker,
  k_spline = 5
)

# =========================================================
# OUTPUTS
# =========================================================

#round_df_4(res_topicloo_linear_vs_pspline$split_results_all)
round_df_4(res_topicloo_linear_vs_pspline$cluster_summary_all)
round_df_4(res_topicloo_linear_vs_pspline$experiment_summary_all)
#round_df_4(res_topicloo_linear_vs_pspline$overall_summary)
```




Für den Topic-LOO-Vergleich wurden Vorhersagen in beiden Modellen erzeugt

Der clustergetrennte Topic-LOO-Vergleich ergab keinen konsistenten Vorhersagevorteil eines gemeinsamen penalized splines gegenüber einem linearen nLetters-Term. Zwar war der Spline technisch in allen Clustern stabil fitbar, seine Vorhersagegüte fiel jedoch über die Experimente hinweg uneinheitlich aus. In einzelnen Datensätzen zeigte sich ein leichter Vorteil, in anderen war der lineare Term gleich gut oder besser, und im CS-Datensatz schnitt der Spline deutlich schlechter ab. Für eine einheitliche Modellspezifikation über alle Experimente hinweg spricht dies gegen den penalized spline als Standardspezifikation und eher für den parsimonischeren linearen nLetters-Term.



Fazit: wir modellieren mit der minimalen Version: nLetters als linearer Koeffizient
